summary.intent: Evolve reflect from a narrow AI session-memory tool into a universal repo knowledge base that compiles all session knowledge into a compounding wiki, queryable via qmd, with no context injection model.
summary.outcome: Successfully implemented and pushed the v1.0.0 knowledge base transformation: qmd made required with auto-install, dynamic wiki categories, broader ingest triage prompt, index.md generation, qmd re-indexing after ingest, and skill rewritten to tell agents to query qmd directly instead of injecting context.md.

repo: Sessions are the sole source of truth — every decision, preference, brand guideline, and correction flows through Entire CLI sessions, making external source adapters unnecessary.
The old model (4 hardcoded wiki categories + context.md injection) is being replaced by: dynamic categories proposed by the triage subagent + agents self-serving via qmd queries.
qmd collection naming uses `reflect-<repo-name>` convention to avoid collisions across multiple repos.
qmd npm package is `@tobilu/qmd`, installs globally via `npm install -g @tobilu/qmd` or `bun install -g @tobilu/qmd`.
Wiki index growth is bounded by lint: stale pages archive to `_archive/`, resolved items move out, duplicates merge — index reflects only active pages.
The skill's new role is to tell agents 'you have memory, query qmd collection reflect-<repo>' rather than reading an injected context.md file.
reflect init is idempotent and now handles qmd install + collection registration as part of setup.
The `.claude/agents/entire-search.md`, `.claude/skills/reflect/SKILL.md`, and `.claude/skills/reflect/hooks/session-start.sh` files were modified by init but not committed — caught by stop hook.

code.finding: Updated to auto-install qmd via npm, register collection as `reflect-<repo>`, and remove context.md injection wiring from `_wire_agents`.

Triage prompt rewritten to extract ALL knowledge types (brand, preferences, patterns, business decisions, etc.) not just 4 session signals. Dynamic category creation: new dirs created on the fly. Added `_qmd_reindex` helper that runs `qmd update` + `qmd embed` after every ingest.
Added `update_index_md()` function that generates a committed, human/LLM-readable table of contents showing only active pages — bounded by lint archiving.
Fully rewritten: agents are told to query qmd collection directly instead of reading context.md. No injection model.
Simplified to only signal REFLECT_WIKI_INGEST — no context generation step.
Bumped to 1.0.0 to mark the knowledge base architecture milestone.

workflow: The pattern 'Reflect writes, qmd reads' cleanly separates concerns — reflect owns ingestion/wiki maintenance, qmd owns retrieval.
Testing qmd auto-install via `reflect init` on an already-initialized repo is safe and idempotent.
Running `reflect init` after skill changes causes Claude Code to hot-reload the skill immediately (visible in system reminders).
Wiping `.reflect/.last_run` forces ingest to reprocess all history — useful for testing new triage prompts against existing session data.
Stop hook correctly caught that init-modified files (.claude/agents/, .claude/skills/) were not staged for commit.

friction: qmd auto-install requires npm global write permissions which may fail in some environments — bun is the fallback.
The existing wiki was built with the old 4-category triage; testing the new broader extraction requires a manual wiki wipe and fresh ingest.
Stop hook flagged uncommitted changes in .claude/ files modified by `reflect init` — these are installation artifacts that need to be committed or gitignored.
open_items: Test the new broader triage prompt against real sessions via wiki wipe + fresh ingest to verify dynamic category creation works as designed.
Test qmd query path end-to-end: `qmd query '<question>' -c reflect-reflect` against the indexed wiki.
Decide whether .claude/agents/ and .claude/skills/ files modified by `reflect init` should be committed to the repo or added to .gitignore.
Wiki compaction strategy (merging related pages when wiki grows large) is noted as future work — not yet implemented.
Global memory layer (`~/.reflect/memory/`) for cross-repo agent preferences was deferred to a future phase.
Multi-output context formats (onboarding, architecture overview) were discussed but not implemented in this phase.
